and you help me get my Chyren git and github repo linked and synced?

content.text:
Your `Chyren` repo is now linked and synced with GitHub.

- `origin` was already correctly set to `https://github.com/Mega-Therion/Chyren.git`
- Local `main` is now tracking `origin/main`
- I pushed your local commit to GitHub
- Sync check is clean: `main` vs `origin/main` is `0 behind / 0 ahead`

You’re fully connected and in sync now.  
If you want, I can also set up a quick “safe sync” command flow for you (`fetch + status + pull --ff-only + push`) so this stays easy going forward.

content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/mega/Chyren/web/update-vercel-keys.sh
@@
+#!/bin/bash
+set -e
+
+echo "Removing old keys..."
+npx vercel env rm GROQ_API_KEY production --yes || true
+npx vercel env rm GEMINI_API_KEY production --yes || true
+npx vercel env rm ANTHROPIC_API_KEY production --yes || true
+npx vercel env rm GROK_API_KEY production --yes || true
+npx vercel env rm OPENAI_API_KEY production --yes || true
+npx vercel env rm OPENAI_API_BASE production --yes || true
+npx vercel env rm OPENAI_MODEL production --yes || true
+
+echo "Adding OpenRouter keys..."
+if [ -z "${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
+  echo "OPENAI_API_KEY is not set in your shell. Export it first and rerun."
+  exit 1
+fi
+echo -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY}" | npx vercel env add OPENAI_API_KEY production
+echo -n "${OPENAI_API_BASE:-https://openrouter.ai/api/v1}" | npx vercel env add OPENAI_API_BASE production
+echo -n "${OPENAI_MODEL:-meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free}" | npx vercel env add OPENAI_MODEL production
+
+echo "Env variables updated successfully."
*** End Patch

content.text: I’m adding a one-command `safe-sync` script now so you can keep local + GitHub aligned without worrying about risky git commands. After that I’ll give you a clear “what’s linked vs what still needs manual auth” dashboard.

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /home/mega/Chyren/ops/scripts/git-safe-sync.sh
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Safe default branch sync:
+# 1) fetch remote
+# 2) fail if local has unstaged/uncommitted changes
+# 3) fast-forward pull only (no merge commits)
+# 4) push current branch
+
+branch="${1:-$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)}"
+remote="${2:-origin}"
+
+echo "==> Sync target: ${remote}/${branch}"
+
+git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null
+
+if [[ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]]; then
+  echo "Working tree is not clean. Commit or stash changes before safe sync."
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+git fetch "$remote"
+git checkout "$branch" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+git pull --ff-only "$remote" "$branch"
+git push "$remote" "$branch"
+
+echo "Safe sync complete: ${branch} is aligned with ${remote}/${branch}."
*** End Patch

input.description: Make safe sync script executable
Show current repository changes after integration fixes

content.text:
Yes — and we made solid progress already. I’m confident handling this with you.

What I’ve done so far to de-risk and stabilize Chyren:

- Confirmed GitHub link/sync is healthy: `main` is tracking `origin/main` and is fully synced (`0 behind / 0 ahead`).
- Confirmed CLI auth:
  - GitHub CLI authenticated as your account
  - Vercel CLI authenticated as your account/team
- Audited Vercel:
  - Found `chyren-web` project linked and active
  - Confirmed key env vars exist there (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_BASE`, `SUPABASE_*`, `OMEGA_DB_URL`, `CRON_SECRET`, etc.)
  - Found `gateway` is **not linked** to a Vercel project yet
- Found and fixed a real deploy blocker in `web/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts` (`prefer-const` lint error).
- Created a safe sync helper script: `ops/scripts/git-safe-sync.sh`
  - It does: fetch -> clean-tree check -> `pull --ff-only` -> push
- Cleaned critical security rough edges:
  - Removed hardcoded API keys from tracked code/scripts:
    - `web/update-vercel-keys.sh`
    - `cortex/chiral_task_renew.py`
    - `cortex/eval_adversarial.py`
    - `cortex/eval_adversarial_stress.py`
    - `cortex/eval_main.py`
    - `ops/docker-compose.yml`

Important things I found:

- Your repo has **no GitHub webhooks** configured directly on the repo.
- Vercel deploy logs show builds are coming from GitHub branch/commit, so Git integration is active on Vercel side.
- Some recent Vercel deploy failures were due:
  - Lint/type issue (I fixed one blocker)
  - Neon quota exceeded during prebuild context fetch
- Your machine hit disk pressure (`ENOSPC`) during local install; I cleared `web/node_modules` to recover space for edits.

## What still needs to be completed (next phase)

I can keep going and do this end-to-end, but a few items require either your platform auth flow or your go-ahead:

- **Rotate compromised keys now** (high priority): keys were previously hardcoded in repo history/files.
- **Link/deploy `gateway` on Vercel** (currently unlinked).
- **Install/auth CLIs or use MCP auth** to verify live project linkage for:
  - Supabase
  - Neon
  - Firebase
  - Cloudflare
- **Normalize env strategy** so one source of truth syncs cleanly to Vercel/GitHub/containers.

## How to use safe sync now

From repo root:

- `./ops/scripts/git-safe-sync.sh`
- Optional custom branch: `./ops/scripts/git-safe-sync.sh main origin`

If you want, I’ll continue immediately with a strict “platform-by-platform completion pass” and produce a final checklist with statuses: `connected`, `misconfigured`, or `fixed` for each service.

content.text:
Push + PR action executed as far as possible under your constraints.

- Created branch: `cursor/integration-hardening`
- Pushed branch to remote: `origin/cursor/integration-hardening`
- Attempted draft PR to `main`

GitHub rejected PR creation with:

- `No commits between main and cursor/integration-hardening`

Reason: the new branch currently points to the same commit as `main`, and your local changes are uncommitted (per your instruction to not stage/commit), so there is nothing for a PR diff.

If you want, I can proceed the moment you allow one of these:
- create a commit from the current local changes on `cursor/integration-hardening`, then open the draft PR, or
- keep no-commit policy and stop here with branch pushed only.

- [ ] Verify Vercel deployment succeeds on this branch
- [ ] Confirm required env vars exist in Vercel project settings
EOF
)"

input.description: Audit repo structure
input.prompt:
You are auditing the repository at /home/mega/Chyren for strategic improvement opportunities. Perform a medium-thorough exploration focused on: architecture clarity, documentation coherence, onboarding/readme quality, test coverage signals, CI/CD quality gates, security hygiene, secrets handling, deployment reliability, and product narrative consistency. Return:
1) Top 20 improvement opportunities grouped by category with concrete file references.
2) For each item: severity (high/med/low), impact, effort (S/M/L), and suggested action.
3) Notable strengths to preserve.
Do not modify files.

content.text:
Yes — and this is exactly the right way to do it.

You’re not “wrong”; you’re at the stage every serious project hits: **stabilize, systematize, and professionalize**.  
I ran a deep repo-level audit and turned it into a focused execution strategy.

## What Chyren Needs Next (Impeccability Backlog)

### P0 — Critical (do first)
1. **Secrets incident response**
- Rotate all provider/database/deploy keys that were ever committed.
- Add mandatory secret scanning in CI (`gitleaks`) so leaks can’t merge again.
- Add a documented “if secret leaked” runbook.

2. **Single source of truth for CI/CD**
- Consolidate duplicate workflow trees (`.github/workflows` vs `ops/.github/workflows`).
- Remove weak gates like `continue-on-error` for critical checks.
- Add required status checks + branch protection expectations.

3. **PR/branch governance**
- Add `CODEOWNERS` for security-sensitive and core areas.
- Enforce review ownership for `web`, `medulla`, `cortex`, and workflows.

4. **Fix architecture/documentation contradictions**
- Establish one canonical “current architecture + target architecture” doc.
- Deconflict overlapping docs (`README`, `CLAUDE`, `GEMINI`, quickstart docs).

---

### P1 — High Value (next wave)
5. **Rewrite Quickstart to reality**
- Build a verified “first 10 minutes” path that actually works today.
- Align commands/paths with current repo structure.

6. **Test strategy expansion**
- Define layered tests: unit, integration, contract, smoke.
- Add minimum coverage gates per stack (Rust/Python/Web) in CI.

7. **Deployment hardening**
- Strengthen Vercel/env sync scripts with preflight validation + dry-run + post-deploy checks.
- Reduce deployment drift and “it worked locally” failures.

8. **E2E reliability**
- Run Playwright against deterministic test envs, not production-only URLs.

---

### P2 — Polish / Proof / Product positioning
9. **Evidence/proof maturity framework**
- Standardize what claims are “implemented”, “experimentally validated”, or “aspirational”.
- Tie architectural claims to measurable acceptance criteria.

10. **Narrative cleanup**
- Separate product docs from personal/brand narrative so external readers onboard faster.

11. **Operational excellence**
- Add SAST/CodeQL, vulnerability triage cadence, release fallback strategy, and health dashboards.

---

## Strategy To Implement (Execution Plan)

## Phase 1: Safety & Integrity Lockdown (Week 1)
- Secret rotation + CI secret scanning + security runbook
- CI/workflow consolidation + hard fail quality gates
- Add `CODEOWNERS` + branch protection checklist docs
- **Deliverable:** “safe to collaborate and deploy” baseline

## Phase 2: Truthful Docs & Reliable Onboarding (Week 2)
- Canonical architecture spec
- Rewrite `docs/QUICKSTART.md`
- Doc link/path validation checks in CI
- **Deliverable:** new user can bootstrap without guesswork

## Phase 3: Verification & Testing Upgrade (Weeks 3–4)
- Coverage gates + missing tests in critical flows
- Contract tests for provider routing/fallback behavior
- Stable E2E path in CI
- **Deliverable:** regressions caught early, confidence before release

## Phase 4: Deployment and Proof Maturity (Weeks 5–6)
- Harden deploy/env sync scripts
- Formal proof/evidence checklist for claims
- Final repo quality pass and release readiness report
- **Deliverable:** auditable, professional-grade repo posture

---

## Definition of “Impeccable” (Acceptance Criteria)
- No hardcoded secrets in repo; secret scan mandatory in CI.
- One clear CI pipeline and explicit required checks.
- One canonical architecture + one trustworthy quickstart.
- Test and coverage floors enforced.
- Deployment scripts validated and repeatable.
- Claims in docs traceable to evidence.

---

If you approve, I’ll start **Phase 1 immediately** and execute end-to-end with clear checkpoints after each phase.
